title: Im gonna be viewing NGE for the first timeWrite ups
display_title: Im gonna be viewing NGE for the first timeWrite ups
wikitext:
[]: I'm gonna be viewing NGE for the first time(Write-ups!)             Quote Post by NickFury90 » Mon Jun 15, 2009 5:18 am Hello everyone! This is my first post here so I hope you guys welcome me with open arms! First, a little background. I always enjoyed a good anime. Like many fans, I grew up watching the decopressed stylings of DBZ. Now that I look back on it I have no idea how I could stand to watch 300 episodes of something that could have been done in 1/4th as many, but I had a great care and understanding for the characters and the stories. I watched a couple more now and again(Outlaw Star was a big favorite as a kid), but it wasn't until I discovered Cowboy Bebop that I really fell in love with the meduim. I obviously don't have to explain Bebop to any of you; its a show that oozes style and pop-cool, but has fantastic characters and plots throughout, and in much much MUCH shorter time than something like DBZ or Inuyasha or Tenchi Muyo(God, that takes me back ). I never got too hardcore into the medium; just watched whatever came on TV, but besides the occasional Lupin TV Special or Miyazaki film, I kinda stopped watching Anime over the past year or two. Luckily, the sheer manly epicness of Gurren Lagann brought me back full force. Which now(if you still with me here) brings me to this post. Neon Genesis Evangelion. A show many call the best anime ever, and several of my friends call a must-watch, even though one of them didn't enjoy it that much(its just that essential). I wanna see what the hype's about. I wanna go in, without any expectations except for it to be good. I wanna discover the characters, I wanna learn the story, and yes, I wanna see just how crazy a mind**** those last two episodes are. So, sometimes today, I will begin from episode one and work my way through to the end, posting impressions for at least one episode per day. I'd like for you guys to give out whatever comments/suggestions you want; heck you can join me if you want! Thanks in advance, and I look forward to become a real Eva Geek!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jun 15, 2009 5:36 am Welcome to the forum! Man, I wish I could go back and experience NGE for the first time again... the only way to get close to that is to vicariously experience it through those that are new to the series so I hope it will be as powerful a work to you as it was for me and so many others. I look forward to hearing your thoughts on the series episode by episode. Just a few words: *Make sure to watch it in Japanese/subbed. The dub sucks terribly. *Watch it in this order: ep. 1-20, 21-24 Director's Cuts, 25-26, End of Evangelion. *Make sure to watch the Platinum Edition and not the old versions as they have visual and audio remastering, the director's cuts, and better subtitling. Oh, and have a bunch of crazy, existential, mindblowing OMGWTF WAS THAT?!!! fun.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Mon Jun 15, 2009 6:08 am Be prepared to be told that everything you say is wrong... then linked to three-four year old geekgasm threads explaining why.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Jun 15, 2009 6:30 am Have fun~~~~

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Mon Jun 15, 2009 7:34 am Good luck to you, poor sir. Now you're in it for life.

[]:             Quote Post by Nathan » Mon Jun 15, 2009 8:37 am This is an excellent idea, I literally just finished watching for the first time myself and I already want to watch it several more times. Plus this reminds me of the "Lost Rewatch" which is one of the geekiest/coolest things on the internet at the moment

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Jun 15, 2009 11:08 am Don't do it! It will only leave you a shattered wreck, a mere shell of what you once were. Your mind will be ruined and ravaged. Good luck comrade, you will need it.

[]:             Quote Post by NickFury90 » Mon Jun 15, 2009 12:33 pm EPISODE #1 IMPRESSIONS I like the theme song. Its not as immediately catchy like Cowboy Bebop or GaoGaiGar, but it'll grow on me. From what I can understand, the world has been screwed up because some big apocalypse thing, and the survivors gotta face off against this Angel mechs. Thing is tough; none of the human weapons do anything! Geez, way to waste a budget. At least kill those damn annoying crickets. Shinji is complaining already. This is gonna be fun. Random anime girl in school uniform appears and disappears. ok. HOLY CRAP GIANT ROBOT GET OUT OF THERE Shinji! Luckily purple-haired chick shows up to save Shinji, even though their car gets owned. Japanese general guys seem pretty upset that their weapons are like attacking Arnold Schwarzenegger with a jar of toothpicks. Even the M2 Mine or whatever was completely useless. LOL Japanese military. Luckily, unfriendly looking guy with the shades and assistant man seem pretty confident in their secret plan. Misato is funny. She's pretty self-absorbed, but isn't above using illegal batteries. Shinji is still being a douchebag. He should totally bang Misato to bring his cool points up. Glasses Guy is Shinji's dad, and is apparently head of NERV, whatever that acronym means. They don't seem to think so highly of each other, thanks to that little flashback image. Misato doesn't have a good relationship with her father too. Hey look a guidebook. OH SNAP UNDERGROUND CITY! Epic music! Misato is totally useless and gets lost. Sexy blond girl gets gratuitous ass shot; thats always a good way to make a first impression. Ritsuko has the whole sexy scientist thing going on, and I assume is above Misato's level. Shinji is in an elevator with two hot girls and his nose is still in the books. So young. Silhouette conversations are always a great way to get away with not having to animate anything. Looks like they're are going to have to defy all odds for the machine to work. Shinji's girly scream when he sees the Eva lost him even more cool points. The machine is super special awesome, and mankind's last hope. Shinji's dad shows up to be a total dickhead. Shinji is suppose to pilot Unit 01 because Rei is injured, even though he just got here. Shinji is being a little coward. "Hey kid, would YOU like a giant robot?" "No dad, I can't do it! HOW COULD YOU DO THIS TO ME I CAN'T DO IT " Shinji is bating a 1000 for the Punkass League. Even Misato stops being light-hearted and tells Shinji to shove his cowardly ways and confront his fears. "Our spare is unusable" Shinji's dad is still a total dickhead. Whoa, Unit 01 protected Shinji all by itself. Rei, even in her injured state, has more balls than Shinji. "I mustn't run away, I mustn't run away, I mustn't run away, I mustn't run away!" Least I know where that joke comes from now. Good lord that "Voltron Combine!" scene took forever. "Connecting main power! "Nerve connection good!" "Removing Primary lock bolts!". Hope they don't do that every episode. WHEE! Up to the surface. C'mon Angel, I'm ready to whoop dat ass! Shinji's dad smiles, apparently knowing more than the audience does. OVERALL IMPRESSIONS: Well I like it so far. Some interesting characters, great mech design, interesting premise. Too bad Shinji has no balls what so ever. Look forward to the next episode!

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Jun 15, 2009 12:45 pm ^ Probably the most accurate description of the series ever. It will be interesting to see the series from the point of view of another. I will continue to follow these updates with interest.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Jun 15, 2009 12:51 pm ^ Major ditto. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Nathan » Mon Jun 15, 2009 1:33 pm Yeah same as the other 2, I enjoyed reading that look forward to the rest

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jun 15, 2009 8:19 pm EPISODE #1 IMPRESSIONS OF YOUR IMPRESSIONS: NickFury90 wrote: I like the theme song.

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Mon Jun 15, 2009 8:49 pm Have Fun. Be wary browsing the "why is Shinji so universally hated" thread, major ending spoilers. Enjoy every episode, and if you understand Shinji's plight, you're gonna get a lot out of this. Don't worry, Shinji is gonna get said 'cool points' in due time.

[]:             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Tue Jun 16, 2009 3:28 am My god, why does everyone hate the dub so much? It wasn't that bad.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jun 16, 2009 3:46 am With most , it's a genuine dislike that isn't really all that pronounced, but the agreements between each other increase things more than necessary. With others, it's the internet-borne belief that "LOL MY OPINONS ARE THE ONLY ONES THAT MATTER" so they shove the original down people's throats. A few others are attempting to look cool. The last set are elitists who get probably upset about the translation from "Kamen" to "Mask". Just ignore them and don't bother debating. After all, they either see you as a person with a genuine like that is only opinion and isn't worth fighting over; an internet fail shoving beliefs down THEIR throats; a groupie, or a jingoist.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Jun 16, 2009 12:45 pm this thread is awesome :)

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Tue Jun 16, 2009 12:58 pm semi spoiler: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by NickFury90 » Tue Jun 16, 2009 1:19 pm EPISODE TWO IMPRESSIONS All right, its go time! No wait, Shinji can't even take his first few steps without literally falling flat on his face. The Angel is probably loling to himself right now. The Angel snaps his arm! Eew, that was creepily realistic. Laser through the face! Don't worry, I'm sure Shinji will get out of thisYEAHBUTWHAT? What the hell just happen? Shinji's in a hospital or something with those annoying ass cicadas again. "An unfamiliar ceiling". The audience is about as clueless as Shinji is to how the fight went down. Japanese General Guys must be a big fan of Simon, because they could play with their desks. Everybody is upset about all the time/money/manpower going into the giant robos when they could be spending it on the human enhancement project, which could mean anything at this point; maybe they want all the female employees to have bigger breasts, or maybe its a penis enlargement thing. Glasses Guy seems pretty contend, knowing he has the biggest penis of all. Rei, in her injured state, gives Shinji the stink eye as she passes by. Shinji looks down at the ground in shame of Rei's prowess over him. Repairs are done throughout the city, government is hiding things; its kinda like real life, just with giant robots and humorous purple haired women. Misato and Shinji run into Glasses Guy. Epic staredown! Shinji looks down at the ground in shame of Glasses Guy's prowess over him. This is becoming a recurring theme. Shinji is fine with being a loner, but Misato is taking him. Shinji! Don't pass up a great opportunity! People at Wal-Mart are talking about last night. It lets the audience get a feel of the what the general public think about all these crazy mecha fights when they're trying to catch Japanese Idol. Epic music time again! The city just comes straight out of the ground, and its totally awesome. Also, apparently Shinji DID save the town, which just don't know how yet. They get to Misato's place, and Shinji is still really anti-social. Holy CRAP, its dirty as hell. Reminds me of my room. Half of Misato's possessions are beer, apparently, which is awesome. "He's probably still sleeping!" She keeps her pet dog in the other fridge? I hope PETA doesn't come to her apartment. Misato walks around in booty shorts and guzzles down beer. Where has this woman been all my life? Shinji is still being uneasy and douchy. Also, gratuitous ass shot #2! Gotta love those. Paper scissors rocks is a great way to split up chores. "Don't take advantage of me!" Sic. Holy shit its a penguin thats been in the fridge! Thats completely wacky and goes with Misato's character. I will make it my life mission to find out if any future girlfriends of mine keep exotic animals in their refrigerators. Glasses Guy is still a total dickhead about everything. He's pretty good at that. LOL tape recorders. The world is so advanced, but they still haven't invented a decent MP3 player. "Another unfamiliar ceiling". Oh snap, flashback time! Now we can finally see how this fight went down. Shinji gets owned, and the Eva takes over for him. That Eva needs to find a good giant robo dentist, or at least put down the cherry Kool-Aid for awhile. Eva RIPS through the ATV field like it was a spiderweb. Giant laser blast shaped like a cross! Oh I get it, angel symbolism. Gotcha. No effect on Eva though, who proceeds to SNAP THE ANGEL'S HANDS! That was hard to watch/listen to. Boot to the face! Eva is raping this dude, and the Angel does the whole "If I go down, I'm taking you with me!" bit. Oh noez! GIANT EXPLOSION! Eva walks through the flames like a total badass. Glasses Guy is contend in this development. Half of the Eva's face falls off, revealing the inner workings of the machine. Holy shit, its another eye! ITS ALIVE, IGOR! GIRLY SCREAM TIME AGAIN! Misato comes into Shinji's bedroom dripping wet, but of course Shinji is totally turned off. I don't want to jump to conclusions about your sexuality but...eh. She congrats him on a job well done today, and tells him to hang in there. OVERALL IMPRESSIONS: Better than the first one. Misato is a cool character. Shinji, while still an anti-social punk, is still very interesting. The story continues to keep my interest, and you can tell their some ominous things going on. Whats the human enhancement program? Whats up with that Rei chick? And what the flying fuck was going on with the Eva having a real eye? Look forward to the next episode!

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jun 16, 2009 1:36 pm I love these comments. Watch more episodes. NOW. *kidnaps Nick and places him in a special Evangelion Observation area*

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 16, 2009 2:04 pm xtr00kvltcorex wrote: semi spoiler:
